name: subtopic
description: Sub-section boundary — lighter than divider, no canvas reskin.
purpose: Introduces a specific topic within a section. Use between divider and content slides for finer orientation. Same light canvas as content slides, centered heading, no special background — a lighter cousin to the dark divider.
sample:
<!-- _class: subtopic -->

`The Framework · Component 02`

## Before we can score a signal, we have to agree on what one is.

whenToUse.title: Between divider and content
whenToUse.body: After the dark divider opens a section, a subtopic narrows the focus to one specific topic before the audience hits the first content slide. Finer-grained orientation.

whenToUse.title: Lighter than divider
whenToUse.body: Same bright canvas as body slides — keeps the visual rhythm intact while still signaling a topic shift. Lighter context-switch cost than a divider.

whenToUse.title: Eyebrow names the topic family
whenToUse.body: The inline-code paragraph above the heading is the breadcrumb. "Section 02 · Module 03" tells the audience exactly where they are in the deck's hierarchy.

antiPatterns.title: Used as a divider replacement
antiPatterns.body: If the next slides are an entirely new section, reach for divider (dark canvas, h1). Subtopic is for sub-section orientation, not section starts.

antiPatterns.title: Stacked subtopics
antiPatterns.body: Two subtopic slides back-to-back means the first didn't introduce anything. Merge them or move directly to content.

antiPatterns.title: h1 instead of h2
antiPatterns.body: The layout expects h2 (subtopic name is one level below the section title). Promoting to h1 makes the slide compete visually with the divider.

related.name: divider
related.when: major section starts — darker, h1, dedicated canvas

related.name: title
related.when: opens the deck — same dark-bookend chrome as divider

related.name: content
related.when: the body slides that follow a subtopic

numbered.label: Numbered — auto-incrementing module index
numbered.caption: Stamps an auto-incrementing module number. Each subtopic carries an independent counter from divider's section counter, so "Section 02 · Module 04" makes sense.
numbered.sample:
<!-- _class: subtopic numbered -->

`Module 04`

## Then the next sub-topic, numbered automatically.
